message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@kumar.ayush-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
Whats the response we are receiving from the server?
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
I will start checking the logs now
Can anyone share the logs
Could it be your checkout change again @Shay? It broke the platform last week!
:star: Ask team members to provide evidence of their assertions
The checkout service logs are healthy and the service is working as intended
Remember to @ characters to get their attention!
I see 504 HTTP errors in the payment service, which means the application is failing to serve the requests
*Incident Number INC100084:* payments not going through Europe seems affected
Payment service health checks are also failing with 504s and latency has increased significantly.
The payment service is consistently returning 504 Gateway Timeout errors upstream.
A 504 error indicates that the payment service is timing out or is unavailable to handle requests.
This suggests the payment service isn't responding in time, so we should check for recent changes or dependencies affecting its responsiveness.
Thanks for the update. I need exact figures—how many customers or orders are impacted? Also, what’s the estimated time to resolution?
Is there any particular IP address which has made many requests?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
I don't see any single IP address making an unusual number of requests in the payment service logs.
Security logs show no evidence of suspicious or excessive traffic from any particular IP address.
Sharing that shortly
Was there any recent change in payment service?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Or any kind of bulk activity going on?
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Any updates on the investigation?
There is no evidence of bulk activity or traffic spikes in the payment service metrics.
what was the reason for java code upgrade?
:star: Reviewed changes
The PaymentDatabase upgrade was to improve indexing and transaction processing, not related to a Java code upgrade.
It seems only two changes were there on the prod, can we first revert it if it has no incremental business impact
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
My code isn't even being called yet, so I don't think rolling back is necessary.
Would make more sense to investigate Shay's change first?
Daniel has a point - let's try Shay's change first before touching the payment service
If payments service seems to be affected, lets revert the payment service change first
I really don't think it's necessary, I did full testing and it passed.
Could it be load related due to the flash sale?
User activity does not seem much, do we see any increased throughput?
Daniel, please rollback your changes now, we don't have time for this.
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
@daniel let us know when that happens?
I mean when you have reverted the changes back
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
Did that fix it?
:bulb: To complete this drill, ask the engineering team to verify the payment service is working, and check with Bob if UK customer payments have recovered.
504s seems decreasing
The rollback is fully done, so we're ready to check if payments are working again.
The changes have already been reverted, so we can proceed with verification now.
:star: Use metrics as evidence
Would you like me to place a test order to confirm the payment service is functioning properly?
I can see the metrics on grafan
Do you want me to run a test transaction to make sure everything is back to normal?
yes @tanya please
I'll check the payment service logs now.
I'll try a test transaction from my side.
Logs are looking clean, no more 504 errors.
My test payment went through successfully! Looks like we're back in business.
User activity has increased too
That's great news! To be sure, can we check with Bob if customer payments are processing normally from the external side?
Everything looks good internally, but let's confirm with Bob that customers aren't seeing any issues now.
Bob can we confirm if orders are going through?
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
I don't think the Beatles would've anticipated cryptocurrency so let's keep it that way for now
Excellent! Get me a full incident report by end of day

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.

If you've already closed the tab, you can reopen the form using the button below.
Great work everyone - crisis averted!
The issue has been resolved, it was due to a recent change for this goal -  Incremental feature release and bug fix to improve card payment reliability, we have reverted the changes back
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
Has revenue flow been fully restored? Please confirm that customers can now complete payments and orders are processing as normal.
yes everything is back to normal now
Can anyone share how much revenue we lost because of this?
And how many users were impacted?

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
https://learn.uptimelabs.io/scenario/online-boutique/levels/5/8085/feedback
Understood. Please send me a summary of the total impact—number of failed transactions and estimated revenue loss. I want this documented for review.
